theme: Lay out data to survive a row and column loss
title: Diagonal Parity Grid
strategies: minimal-covering-diagonal baseline: raw cells only along a covering diagonal (few raw cells), rest parity with injective Cauchy tags -- safe but wasteful

spread layout + collision-prone coefficient tags (small modulus reused across many parity cells): keeps a near-maximal raw count but loses independence (rank) on larger grids where the tag range is smaller than the number of parity cells

spread layout + globally-injective Cauchy/Vandermonde-style coefficient tags (distinct field element per cell, e.g. linear index): guarantees full rank for every row+column erasure pattern simultaneously, hitting the combinatorial rank bound with a near-maximal raw count
row+column reservation with per-cell weighting / exchange search over which cells to sacrifice as parity
